Schema.Title: Food/Beverage/Tobacco
Schema.Definition: Includes all food, beverage and tobacco products intended for human consumption.

Childs.Title: Beverages
Childs.Definition: Includes all products that are intended to be consumed as a drink.
Childs.DefinitionExcludes: Excludes products such as non-drinkable liquids.

Childs.Title: Hot Beverages
Childs.Definition: Includes all products that are typically consumed as a warm or hot drink.

Childs.Title: Instant Coffee/Coffee Substitutes
Childs.Definition: Includes any products that can be described/observed as a soluble powder or granules derived from brewed coffee, intended to be dissolved in hot water. Includes products such as Instant Coffee, Coffee Granules and Coffee Substitutes.
Childs.DefinitionExcludes: Excludes Ground Coffee and Whole Bean Coffee classified elsewhere.

Childs.Title: Type of Instant Coffee/Coffee Substitute
Childs.Definition: Indicates, with reference to the product branding, labelling or packaging, the descriptive term that is used by the product manufacturer to identify the type of instant coffee or coffee substitute.

Childs.Title: UNCLASSIFIED
Childs.Definition: This term is used to describe those product attributes that are unable to be classified within their specific market.

Childs.Title: UNIDENTIFIED
Childs.Definition: This term is used to describe those product attributes that are unidentifiable given existing or available product information.

Childs.Title: Ground Coffee
Childs.Definition: Includes any products that can be described/observed as roasted coffee beans reduced to small particles for use in a coffee maker or filter. Includes products such as Espresso Ground Coffee and Filter Ground Coffee.
Childs.DefinitionExcludes: Excludes Whole Bean Coffee and Instant Coffee classified elsewhere.

Childs.Title: Type of Ground Coffee
Childs.Definition: Indicates, with reference to the product branding, labelling or packaging, the descriptive term that is used by the product manufacturer to identify the type of ground coffee.

Childs.Title: UNCLASSIFIED
Childs.Definition: This term is used to describe those product attributes that are unable to be classified within their specific market.

Childs.Title: UNIDENTIFIED
Childs.Definition: This term is used to describe those product attributes that are unidentifiable given existing or available product information.

Childs.Title: Dairy Products/Eggs
Childs.Definition: Includes all products that are produced from milk or eggs.
Childs.DefinitionExcludes: Excludes Dairy Alternatives classified in Beverages.

Childs.Title: Milk
Childs.Definition: Includes all products that are classified as a white liquid produced by the mammary glands of mammals, used as a food by humans.

Childs.Title: Fresh Milk
Childs.Definition: Includes any products that can be described/observed as pasteurised or homogenised milk sold fresh with a short shelf life. Includes Whole Milk, Semi-Skimmed Milk and Skimmed Milk.
Childs.DefinitionExcludes: Excludes UHT Milk, Powdered Milk and Condensed Milk classified elsewhere.

Childs.Title: Type of Fresh Milk
Childs.Definition: Indicates, with reference to the product branding, labelling or packaging, the descriptive term that is used by the product manufacturer to identify the fat content or type of fresh milk.

Childs.Title: UNCLASSIFIED
Childs.Definition: This term is used to describe those product attributes that are unable to be classified within their specific market.

Childs.Title: UNIDENTIFIED
Childs.Definition: This term is used to describe those product attributes that are unidentifiable given existing or available product information.
